circuit:Abu Dhabi.title: Yas Marina Circuit
circuit:Abu Dhabi.summary: The Yas Marina Circuit, a 5. 281 km track on Yas Island in Abu Dhabi designed by Hermann Tilke, has been the venue for Formula One's season finale since 2009. It represents the second Middle Eastern Formula One circuit, following Bahrain and preceding facilities in Qatar and Saudi Arabia.

circuit:Australia.title: Albert Park Circuit
circuit:Australia.summary: The Albert Park Circuit is a Melbourne street course that loops around Albert Park Lake and hosts the Formula One Australian Grand Prix each year, along with various supporting motorsport events. The track holds the highest FIA safety classification.

circuit:Austria.title: Red Bull Ring
circuit:Austria.summary: Located in Spielberg, Austria, this 4. 326 km circuit has a rich Formula One history spanning two distinct eras. Originally established as the Österreichring, it held the Austrian Grand Prix continuously from 1970 to 1987 before undergoing significant modifications and rebranding as the A1-Ring, which then hosted the race from 1997 to 2003.

circuit:Azerbaijan.title: Baku City Circuit
circuit:Azerbaijan.summary: The Baku City Circuit is a street course in Azerbaijan's capital city, stretching 6. 003 kilometers and ranking as the fourth-longest venue on the Formula One schedule. The track hosted its first F1 race in 2016 as the European Grand Prix before becoming a permanent fixture on the calendar the following year under its current name, the Azerbaijan Grand Prix.

circuit:Bahrain.title: Bahrain International Circuit
circuit:Bahrain.summary: Opened in 2004, the Bahrain International Circuit stretches 5. 412 km and earned distinction as the venue for Formula One's inaugural Middle Eastern grand prix. Beyond its flagship F1 event, the FIA Grade 1-licensed facility hosts various racing series including drag racing, GP2, and previously Australian V8 Supercars, while also staging 24-hour endurance competitions across multiple circuit configurations.

circuit:Barcelona.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Barcelona.summary: Located in Montmeló near Barcelona, the Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya spans 4. 657 kilometers and features a balanced mix of extended straights and diverse corner configurations, making it a versatile venue for competitive racing. The track holds FIA Grade 1 certification and can accommodate over 140,000 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:Belgium.title: Circuit de Spa-Francorchamps
circuit:Belgium.summary: The Belgian Grand Prix takes place at this 7. 004 km circuit nestled in the Walloon region near the town of Spa, which has hosted Formula One racing since 1950 and continuously since 1985 with only two exceptions. Known for its challenging high-speed layout, the venue remains one of the sport's most prestigious and historically significant racing destinations.

circuit:Canada.title: Circuit Gilles Villeneuve
circuit:Canada.summary: Located on Notre Dame Island near Montreal, this 4. 361 km temporary street circuit hosts Canada's Formula One Grand Prix. Beyond its role in motorsport's premier series, the track has accommodated various international racing competitions including sportscar championships and North American racing series.

circuit:China.title: Shanghai International Circuit
circuit:China.summary: Shanghai International Circuit in China's Jiading District hosts the Chinese Grand Prix, which ran continuously from 2004 until 2019 before resuming in 2024. The track has expanded its motorsport calendar to include electric racing events starting in 2024.

circuit:Great Britain.title: Silverstone Circuit
circuit:Great Britain.summary: Located in England near Silverstone and Whittlebury in Northamptonshire, this circuit has been the traditional home of the British Grand Prix since 1948 and notably staged the inaugural World Championship race in 1950. After sharing hosting duties with Aintree and Brands Hatch between 1955 and 1986, the grand prix became a permanent fixture at the track from 1987 onwards, which also regularly features in the MotoGP calendar.

circuit:Hungary.title: Hungaroring
circuit:Hungary.summary: The Hungaroring, a 4. 381 km circuit near Budapest in Hungary, hosted the first Formula One Grand Prix held in Eastern Europe when it opened in 1986. Built in just eight months, the track was constructed outside the city as a compromise after initial plans for a street circuit in Budapest's central park were rejected by authorities.

circuit:Italy.title: Autodromo Nazionale Monza
circuit:Italy.summary: The Autodromo Nazionale Monza is a 5. 793 km Italian racing circuit established in 1922 near Milan, making it Europe's oldest purpose-built track. Home to the Italian Grand Prix continuously since 1949, the venue earned its "Temple of Speed" nickname for its extended straights and fast-flowing corners that encourage high speeds.

circuit:Japan.title: Suzuka International Racing Course
circuit:Japan.summary: Suzuka Circuit, a 5. 807 km Japanese motorsport venue situated in Mie Prefecture and operated by Honda Mobilityland, serves as a premier destination for Formula One racing. The track, which accommodates 155,000 spectators, has become synonymous with high-profile international championships and stands as one of the sport's most iconic racing facilities.

circuit:Las Vegas.title: Las Vegas Strip Circuit
circuit:Las Vegas.summary: The Las Vegas Strip Circuit is a street course that navigates through Paradise, Nevada, weaving around the famous Las Vegas Boulevard and its surrounding hospitality venues. The track passes by iconic landmarks such as MSG Sphere, Caesars Palace, Bellagio, and Paris Las Vegas, making it one of motorsport's most visually distinctive urban circuits.

circuit:Mexico City.title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
circuit:Mexico City.summary: This 4. 304 km Mexico City circuit commemorates brothers Ricardo and Pedro Rodríguez, both accomplished racing drivers who tragically died in motorsport accidents in 1962 and 1971 respectively. The venue returned as a Formula One host in 2015 following earlier stints on a different configuration, with its most recent prior race occurring three decades earlier in 1992.

circuit:Miami.title: Miami International Autodrome
circuit:Miami.summary: Miami's Formula One venue is a temporary circuit constructed around Hard Rock Stadium in Florida, measuring 3. 363 miles with 19 corners and an average qualifying speed near 139 mph. Designed by specialist circuit architects, the track joined the championship calendar in 2022 as the home of the Miami Grand Prix.

circuit:Monaco.title: Circuit de Monaco
circuit:Monaco.summary: The Circuit de Monaco is a 3. 337 km street track that winds through Monte Carlo and La Condamine districts, encircling Monaco's harbor. The layout follows the city's existing roads throughout the principality, and is frequently known by the name of its primary neighborhood, Monte Carlo.

circuit:Netherlands.title: Circuit Zandvoort
circuit:Netherlands.summary: Located in the Dutch coastal dunes 35 kilometers west of Amsterdam, Zandvoort is a 4. 259-kilometer track that resumed hosting Formula One racing in 2021 with the revival of the Dutch Grand Prix. The circuit's involvement in the championship will conclude following the 2026 season.

circuit:Qatar.title: Lusail International Circuit
circuit:Qatar.summary: Situated north of Doha in Qatar, the Lusail International Circuit spans 5. 419 kilometers with a 1. 068-kilometer main straight and holds an FIA Grade 1 license.

circuit:São Paulo.title: Autódromo José Carlos Pace
circuit:São Paulo.summary: Interlagos is a 4. 309 km Brazilian racing circuit in São Paulo that opened in 1940 and was renamed in 1985 to commemorate Formula One driver José Carlos Pace, who perished in an aviation accident eight years earlier. The track operates in a counterclockwise direction and hosts a separate karting facility dedicated to fellow Brazilian racing legend Ayrton Senna.

circuit:Saudi Arabia.title: Jeddah Corniche Circuit
circuit:Saudi Arabia.summary: The Jeddah Corniche Circuit is a street racing venue situated along the Red Sea waterfront in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, which has served as the home of the Formula One Saudi Arabian Grand Prix since its debut in 2021 and began hosting a Formula E event in 2025.

circuit:Singapore.title: Marina Bay Street Circuit
circuit:Singapore.summary: The Marina Bay Street Circuit winds through Singapore's Marina Bay area, incorporating sections of the Downtown Core and Kallang planning districts into its street-based layout.

circuit:Spain.title: Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya
circuit:Spain.summary: Located near Barcelona in Catalonia, the 4. 657-kilometer Circuit de Barcelona-Catalunya features a balanced mix of lengthy straightaways and diverse corner configurations, making it a comprehensive test of racing performance. The venue holds FIA Grade 1 certification and accommodates approximately 140,700 spectators across its grandstand facilities.

circuit:United States.title: Circuit of the Americas
circuit:United States.summary: The Circuit of the Americas in Austin, Texas is a 3. 426-mile FIA Grade 1 facility that serves as the primary venue for the United States Grand Prix in Formula One. Beyond F1, the track hosts major motorsport events including NASCAR, MotoGP's Motorcycle Grand Prix of the Americas, and the Lone Star Le Mans endurance race, while previously accommodating various other racing series and championships.

driver:ALB.title: Alexander Albon
driver:ALB.summary: Thai-British driver Alexander Albon currently races for Williams in Formula One, competing under the Thai national flag.

driver:ALO.title: Fernando Alonso
driver:ALO.summary: Spanish driver Fernando Alonso currently races for Aston Martin in Formula One, where he has claimed two world championships with Renault in 2005 and 2006 alongside 32 Grand Prix victories over 23 seasons. Beyond single-seaters, Alonso achieved significant success in endurance racing, including two Le Mans victories with Toyota and the 2018-19 World Endurance Championship, making him the only driver to win both a Formula One world title and a World Endurance championship.

driver:ANT.title: Andrea Kimi Antonelli
driver:ANT.summary: Italian driver Andrea Kimi Antonelli made his Formula One debut in 2025 and currently races for Mercedes, having secured five race victories to date.

driver:BEA.title: Oliver Bearman
driver:BEA.summary: Oliver Bearman is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. He has established himself as a professional competitor in the sport's top tier.

driver:BOR.title: Gabriel Bortoleto
driver:BOR.summary: Brazilian driver Gabriel Bortoleto currently races in Formula One for the Audi team, competing at the highest level of motorsport after progressing through lower racing categories.

driver:BOT.title: Valtteri Bottas
driver:BOT.summary: Finnish driver Valtteri Bottas has secured two World Championship runner-up finishes during his time with Mercedes in 2019 and 2020, accumulating 10 race victories throughout his 13-year Formula One career. He currently competes for Cadillac in the championship.

driver:COL.title: Franco Colapinto
driver:COL.summary: Franco Colapinto is an Argentine motorsport competitor currently racing in Formula One with the Alpine team. He represents his country on the sport's highest competitive stage.

driver:DOO.title: Jack Doohan (racing driver)
driver:DOO.summary: Jack Doohan is an Australian driver currently serving as a reserve for Haas in Formula One, having made appearances at seven race events between 2024 and 2025. Outside of his F1 commitments, he competes in the European Le Mans Series' LMP2 category with Nielsen Racing.

driver:GAS.title: Pierre Gasly
driver:GAS.summary: Pierre Gasly is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Alpine. The talented driver previously achieved a notable victory at the 2020 Italian Grand Prix while competing for AlphaTauri.

driver:HAD.title: Isack Hadjar
driver:HAD.summary: Isack Alexandre Hadjar, a driver holding both French and Algerian nationality, represents France in Formula One competition with Red Bull Racing.

driver:HAM.title: Lewis Hamilton
driver:HAM.summary: Lewis Hamilton is a British Formula One driver currently racing for Ferrari, holding an unprecedented seven world championship titles that match Michael Schumacher's record. He leads the sport in multiple statistical categories including race victories, pole positions, and podium appearances.

driver:HUL.title: Nico Hülkenberg
driver:HUL.summary: German driver Nicolas Hülkenberg currently races for Audi in Formula One. Beyond his single-seater career, he achieved significant success in endurance motorsport, notably winning the prestigious 24 Hours of Le Mans in 2015 driving for Porsche.

driver:LAW.title: Liam Lawson
driver:LAW.summary: Liam Lawson, a New Zealand-born racing driver, currently represents Racing Bulls in Formula One competition.

driver:LEC.title: Charles Leclerc
driver:LEC.summary: The Monégasque driver Charles Leclerc currently races for Ferrari in Formula One, where he has secured eight Grand Prix victories over his nine-season career. He finished as championship runner-up in 2022 while competing for the Scuderia.

driver:MAG.title: Kevin Magnussen
driver:MAG.summary: Kevin Magnussen, a Danish driver competing in the World Endurance Championship for BMW and part-time in NASCAR, has participated in Formula One across two separate periods, racing from 2014 to 2020 and returning for the 2022 to 2024 seasons.

driver:NOR.title: Lando Norris
driver:NOR.summary: Lando Norris, a British driver for McLaren, claimed the Formula One drivers' title in 2025 after securing 11 race victories throughout his eight-year career in the sport.

driver:OCO.title: Esteban Ocon
driver:OCO.summary: Esteban Ocon is a French Formula One driver currently racing for Haas. Earlier in his career, he achieved his sole Grand Prix victory at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix while driving for Alpine.

driver:PER.title: Sergio Pérez
driver:PER.summary: Sergio "Checo" Pérez represents Mexico in Formula One, currently driving for Cadillac. The experienced racer finished as championship runner-up in 2023 while with Red Bull and has accumulated six Grand Prix victories throughout his 15-year career in the sport.

driver:PIA.title: Oscar Piastri
driver:PIA.summary: Oscar Piastri is an Australian driver currently racing for McLaren in Formula One, having claimed nine Grand Prix victories over his four seasons in the sport.

driver:RIC.title: Daniel Ricciardo
driver:RIC.summary: Daniel Ricciardo, an Australian driver known as "the Honey Badger," participated in Formula One racing from 2011 through 2024, securing eight Grand Prix victories during his 14-year career in the sport.

driver:RUS.title: George Russell (racing driver)
driver:RUS.summary: British driver George Russell currently races for Mercedes in Formula One, having accumulated seven race victories throughout his eight-season career in the sport.

driver:SAI.title: Carlos Sainz Jr.
driver:SAI.summary: Spanish driver Carlos Sainz competes in Formula One for Williams and has secured four Grand Prix victories throughout his 12-season career in the sport.

driver:SAR.title: Logan Sargeant
driver:SAR.summary: American driver Logan Sargeant competed in Formula One during the 2023 and 2024 seasons before transitioning to other racing series. He currently races in the World Endurance Championship's LMGT3 category with Proton Competition and previously competed in IMSA's LMP2 class.

driver:STR.title: Lance Stroll
driver:STR.summary: Lance Stroll is a Canadian Formula One competitor currently driving for Aston Martin in the sport's top racing series.

driver:TSU.title: Yuki Tsunoda
driver:TSU.summary: Yuki Tsunoda is a Japanese racing driver who holds a reserve position with both Red Bull Racing and Racing Bulls. He competed in Formula One across a five-year span from 2021 to 2025.

driver:VER.title: Max Verstappen
driver:VER.summary: The Dutch-Belgian driver competes for Red Bull Racing and has secured four consecutive world championship titles between 2021 and 2024. Across his 12-season Formula One career, Verstappen has accumulated 71 Grand Prix victories.

driver:ZHO.title: Zhou Guanyu
driver:ZHO.summary: Zhou Guanyu is a Chinese racing driver currently serving as a reserve driver for Cadillac in Formula One. He competed in the sport's top category from 2022 through 2024 and holds the distinction of being the sole Chinese driver to have participated in the championship.

team:Alpine.title: Alpine F1 Team
team:Alpine.summary: Alpine Racing operates from its Enstone base as Renault's works team, having been rebranded from Renault F1 Team in 2021 to showcase the French manufacturer's sports car division. The team secured its only Alpine-era victory through Esteban Ocon at the 2021 Hungarian Grand Prix before Renault's departure from the sport at the end of 2025. Currently powered by Mercedes engines and competing under French licensing, the squad continues as a competitive midfield contender in the championship.

team:Aston Martin.title: Aston Martin in Formula One
team:Aston Martin.summary: Aston Martin, a British manufacturer, has engaged in Formula One competition across multiple incarnations, with its present iteration operating under AMR GP Limited as the Aston Martin Aramco Formula One Team.

team:Ferrari.title: Scuderia Ferrari
team:Ferrari.summary: Scuderia Ferrari, the racing arm of the Italian luxury automaker, stands as Formula One's most enduring and accomplished team, participating continuously since the championship's inaugural 1950 season. The squad, affectionately dubbed "the Prancing Horse" after its iconic emblem, has maintained its presence through every era of the sport's history.

team:Haas F1 Team.title: Haas F1 Team
team:Haas F1 Team.summary: Gene Haas founded this American-licensed Formula One team in 2014, with the squad entering the championship in 2016. Ayao Komatsu currently leads the team as principal, taking over from Guenther Steiner, who held the position since the team's establishment before departing in early 2024.

team:Kick Sauber.title: Sauber Motorsport
team:Kick Sauber.summary: Founded in 1970 by Peter Sauber, this Swiss engineering and racing outfit established itself through endurance competition, notably winning Le Mans in 1989 before joining Formula One in 1993. The team competed as an independent constructor through numerous transformations until withdrawing from the sport in 2025 as its fourth-longest-serving participant by race entries. Audi acquired the operation in 2024 to serve as the technical and competitive foundation for its own F1 entry.

team:McLaren.title: McLaren
team:McLaren.summary: McLaren Racing is a British Formula One outfit headquartered in Woking that operates as a subsidiary of the McLaren Group under the ownership of Mumtalakat Holding Company.

team:Mercedes.title: Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team
team:Mercedes.summary: Mercedes has maintained a continuous presence in Formula One since 1954 as both a constructor and engine supplier, with the current team operating from Brackley under a German licence. The squad, now known as Mercedes-AMG Petronas, experienced a significant ownership restructuring in 2022 when Ineos acquired an equal stake alongside Mercedes-Benz Group and team principal Toto Wolff. The marque's racing operations are traditionally associated with the "Silver Arrows" designation.

team:Racing Bulls.title: Racing Bulls
team:Racing Bulls.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One team and constructor that began competing in the 2024 season as Red Bull GmbH's second constructor alongside Red Bull Racing. Based in Faenza with a supporting facility in Milton Keynes, the team operates as part of the Austrian conglomerate's dual-team structure in the sport.

team:RB.title: Racing Bulls
team:RB.summary: Visa Cash App Racing Bulls is an Italian Formula One constructor that began competing in 2024 as part of Red Bull's multi-team operation. Based in Faenza with operations in Milton Keynes, the team operates as Red Bull's second Formula One entity alongside Red Bull Racing.

team:Red Bull Racing.title: Red Bull Racing
team:Red Bull Racing.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing, operating under an Austrian licence from its English base, emerged as a Formula One competitor in 2005 and has become one of two premier-category teams owned by Red Bull GmbH. Christian Horner stewarded the outfit from its inception through 2024, after which Laurent Mekies assumed leadership of the squad.

team:Williams.title: Williams Racing
team:Williams.summary: Williams Grand Prix Engineering is a British Formula One constructor founded in 1977 by Frank Williams and Patrick Head, emerging from the ashes of Williams's earlier racing venture. Based in Grove, Oxfordshire, the team competes in the 2026 season as Atlassian Williams F1 Team and has established itself as a significant force in the sport's history.
